element_introduction_order.mastery_context: players learn team composition through arcade mode progression against increasingly skilled teams
allows strategic customization after players understand core 3v3 mechanics

element_introduction_order.element: Rival Team (Iori Yagami, Billy Kane, Eiji Kisaragi)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: introduces new character archetypes and Kyo's new narrative rival in Iori
story revelation that Kyo must fight his own father; hints at larger plot involving Orochi power

element_introduction_order.first_appears: after defeating Saisyu in arcade mode
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: culmination encounter; Rugal empowered by Orochi demon force, rewards mastery of all previous team battles
improved reaction system allows skilled defense play throughout tournament

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: escalating tournament bracket structure with story reveals at mid-point (Saisyu) and climax (Omega Rugal); tension builds through successive team battles toward narrative confrontation

difficulty_curve.early_game: introductory matches against standard teams to establish baseline 3v3 combat proficiency
difficulty_curve.mid_game: progression against increasingly skilled pre-defined teams; story beat with Saisyu encounter adds narrative tension alongside mechanical difficulty
difficulty_curve.late_game: culmination in Omega Rugal fight requiring mastery of team synergy, dodge/counterattack mechanics, and resource management across three characters
notable_spikes: Saisyu Kusanagi encounter introduces story-driven difficulty spike with paternal conflict narrative
Omega Rugal final boss features enhanced Orochi power, creating peak difficulty requirement

notable_level_patterns.description: Player team must defeat all opponent teams sequentially in arcade mode; each victory progresses toward boss encounters. Eight pre-defined teams (Hero, Fatal Fury, Art of Fighting, Ikari Warriors, Psycho Soldier, Korea, Women Fighters, Rivals) with variable order presentation
Saisyu Kusanagi appears as mid-tournament gate revealing plot information about brainwashing and Rugal's survival; serves narrative transition before final boss
Omega Rugal encounter as enhanced version of previous game's antagonist; represents Orochi Saga narrative beginning with Rugal's new demonic power source
